name: Flatpak
description: Next-generation technology for building and distributing sandboxed desktop applications on Linux.

seo.title: Flatpak — CI/CD for Applications
seo.meta_description: Flatpak: Next-generation technology for building and distributing sandboxed desktop applications on Linux.

when_to_use: Build sandboxed Linux apps independent of host distro
Single build target deployed across Ubuntu, Fedora, Arch
Runtime-based dependency isolation for desktop apps
OCI-compatible image distribution via Flatpak bundles

pros: Sandbox and runtime model improve desktop security
Portals give a clean permission model
Backed by GNOME, Fedora, and major distros
cons: Disk usage from runtimes can be large
Developers must learn manifest and portal concepts
Startup time heavier than native packages
review.verdict: The best-argued future of Linux desktop distribution, if you accept the runtime overhead.
review.best_for: GUI Linux apps wanting modern sandboxing and cross-distro reach.
review.not_for: Server-side, headless, or tightly system-integrated software.

pricing.high_water_mark_reason: Free/open-source — no commercial pricing, no HWM.
